title: Dijital Oyun Tasarımı Bölümü - Bölüm Başkanı Mesajı
content:
Bölümümüz,

Dear Esteemed Students of the Department of Digital Game Design,

The

Department of Digital Game Design

, operating under the Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences at Istanbul Esenyurt University, carries out its educational activities with an academic vision that integrates contemporary design approaches with technological development. It is a pleasure to welcome you to our department.

Today, digital games have evolved beyond entertainment and have become a multidisciplinary field intersecting with

creative industries, cultural production, interaction design, and experience-based media

. Digital Game Design combines art, technology, and narrative to create meaningful interactive experiences.

production- and experience-oriented educational model

, enabling students to integrate theoretical knowledge with practical application. The curriculum includes applied studies in game mechanics, narrative design, visual communication, user experience, prototyping processes, and game engines.

Our qualified academic staff aim not only to provide technical competence but also to develop students’

critical thinking, creative problem-solving, interdisciplinary perspective, and teamwork skills

. Small class sizes allow for individualized academic guidance and creative mentoring.

The department emphasizes university–industry collaboration through workshops, seminars, game development events, internships, and professional engagements, ensuring that students gain sectoral experience during their education.

Istanbul Esenyurt University offers a comprehensive learning environment that supports students academically, socially, culturally, and artistically. Within this framework, the Department of Digital Game Design aims to educate graduates who are well-equipped for both academic and professional careers.

I wish all our students a successful academic journey and look forward to witnessing their creative contributions to the future of digital game design.

Assistant Professor Kevser Soydan
